name: Richard Davis
abstract: Richard Davis (April 15, 1930 – September 6, 2023) was an American jazz bassist. Among his best-known contributions to the albums of others are Eric Dolphy's Out to Lunch!, Andrew Hill's Point of Departure, and Van Morrison's Astral Weeks, of which critic Greil Marcus wrote (in The Rolling Stone Illustrated History of Rock and Roll), "Ri…

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Temperley
about_quotes.evidence: When I actually played with the Thad Jones and Mel Lewis Band... looking at... Richard Davis... and all these people that were in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Temperley interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Tom McIntosh
about_quotes.evidence: I had Art Farmer, James Moody, and myself on the front line. I had Tootie Heath on the drums, Tommy Flanagan and Richard Davis on bass.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom McIntosh — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: I played years ago with Richard Davis, Carl Allen, Buddy Montgomery and myself and Ben Sidran for Ricky Lee Jones.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 18, 2023 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: Richard Davis on the bass... myself. And we were the young musicians in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Gene Bertoncini
about_quotes.evidence: And the rhythm section for Peter Matz and the ‘Kraft Music Hall” was Derek Smith, Mel Lewis and Richard Davis and myself. And all the dates with Don Elliott in recording dates on Madison Avenue, it was always Mel Lewis and Richard Davis and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gene Bertoncini interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Jones Hank
about_quotes.evidence: the thing that I did with Elvin and Richard Davis... I had done a couple of CDs; one was with my brother Elvin and Richard Davis, the bass player. They made – – I think they made two releases from that date.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jones Hank — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
